initial_program:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Manhattan distance to colour 3
dist3(R, C, D) :- input(SR, SC, 3),
                  RD = #max{R - SR, SR - R},
                  CD = #max{C - SC, SC - C},
                  D = RD + CD.

% Manhattan distance to colour 2
dist2(R, C, D) :- input(SR, SC, 2),
                  RD = #max{R - SR, SR - R},
                  CD = #max{C - SC, SC - C},
                  D = RD + CD.

% Minimal distances for each colour
d3_min(R, C, D3) :- #min{ D : dist3(R, C, D) }.
d2_min(R, C, D2) :- #min{ D : dist2(R, C, D) }.

% Exactly one colour per output cell (choose among 0, 2, 3)
1 { output(R, C, Color) : 0, 2, 3 } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Enforce the nearestnonzero rule (colour3 wins ties)
constraint
    :- output(R, C, 2), d3_min(R, C, D3), d2_min(R, C, D2), D3 <= D2.
constraint
    :- output(R, C, 3), d3_min(R, C, D3), d2_min(R, C, D2), D2 < D3.

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Manhattan distance to colour 3
dist3(R, C, D) :- input(SR, SC, 3),
                  RD = #max{R - SR, SR - R},
                  CD = #max{C - SC, SC - C},
                  D = RD + CD.

% Manhattan distance to colour 2
dist2(R, C, D) :- input(SR, SC, 2),
                  RD = #max{R - SR, SR - R},
                  CD = #max{C - SC, SC - C},
                  D = RD + CD.

% Minimal distances for each colour
d3_min(R, C, D3) :- #min{ D : dist3(R, C, D) }.
d2_min(R, C, D2) :- #min{ D : dist2(R, C, D) }.

% Exactly one colour per output cell (choose among 0, 2, 3)
% Exactly one colour per output cell (choose among 0, 2, 3)
% End of exact colour constraint

% Enforce the nearestnonzero rule (colour3 wins ties)
constraint
    :- output(R, C, 2), d3_min(R, C, D3), d2_min(R, C, D2), D3 <= D2.
constraint
    :- output(R, C, 3), d3_min(R, C, D3), d2_min(R, C, D2), D2 < D3.

#show output/3.

% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Manhattan distance to colour 3
dist3(R, C, D) :- input(SR, SC, 3),
                  RD = #max{R - SR, SR - R},
                  CD = #max{C - SC, SC - C},
                  D = RD + CD.

% Manhattan distance to colour 2
dist2(R, C, D) :- input(SR, SC, 2),
                  RD = #max{R - SR, SR - R},
                  CD = #max{C - SC, SC - C},
                  D = RD + CD.

% Minimal distances for each colour
d3_min(R, C, D3) :- #min{ D : dist3(R, C, D) }.
d2_min(R, C, D2) :- #min{ D : dist2(R, C, D) }.

% Exactly one colour per output cell (choose among 0, 2, 3)
% Exactly one colour per output cell (choose among 0, 2, 3)
% End of exact colour constraint

% Enforce the nearestnonzero rule (colour3 wins ties)
constraint
    :- output(R, C, 2), d3_min(R, C, D3), d2_min(R, C, D2), D3 <= D2.

    :- output(R, C, 3), d3_min(R, C, D3), d2_min(R, C, D2), D2 < D3.
